evals.expected_output: Enters preserve mode, establishes a baseline, names the behavior contract, adds characterization tests where needed, introduces seams before moving code, and ships one reviewable slice.
assertions: Includes baseline checks reported as pass/fail/skipped.
Adds or relies on characterization/regression tests before risky movement.
Keeps the change to one reviewable slice rather than a rewrite.

evals.prompt: /m_code_refactor This legacy module has no tests. Clean it up.
evals.expected_output: Starts by characterizing current behavior with tests rather than rewriting, then refactors one small slice.

evals.prompt: /m_code_refactor The order service has business logic in the HTTP controller and talks to the DB directly. Improve the architecture.
evals.expected_output: Enters may-change mode, maps the structure (optionally via the context-scout agent), extracts pure decision logic, introduces a port/seam for persistence, and justifies each abstraction by concrete friction.
assertions: Identifies concrete problems with evidence (controller business logic, direct DB calls).
Proposes the smallest useful target shape, not a full redesign.
Justifies each new abstraction against the decision rules.
Adds tests or seams before risky moves.
Flags auth/migration/etc. for human review where relevant.

evals.prompt: /m_code_refactor Review the architecture of the payments package and give me a migration plan. Don't change anything yet.
evals.expected_output: report-only strategy: an assessment plus a prioritized migration plan, with no code edits.
assertions: Produces findings with severity and evidence.
Produces an ordered migration plan of small steps.
